title: n8n Docker container not accessible from network
symptom: Users see an error message stating 'Could not connect to server' when trying to access the n8n web interface. The Docker logs indicate that the editor is accessible via localhost, which is not reachable from other devices.
applies_when: Running n8n in a Docker container
not_this_if: n8n is accessible from the same machine
root_cause: The n8n instance inside the Docker container is configured to listen only on localhost, making it inaccessible from external devices. This is a common issue when the Docker network mode is set to 'bridge', which isolates the container's network from the host.
fix_steps: Change the Docker network mode to 'host' or configure the container to listen on all interfaces.
Ensure that the port forwarding settings on the Synology and router are correctly set to forward requests to the n8n container.
agent_instruction: Adjust the Docker network settings to allow external access to the n8n container.
